name: I Ching hexagram ob3ect: the 64 hexagrams of the Book of Changes, each composed of 6 lines that may be solid (yang) or broken (yin), with moving lines that transform. FSPLIT: yarrow stalk sorting splits the moment into moving lines (old yin, old yang) and static lines (young yin, young yang) — the moving lines generate a second hexagram from the first. FFUSE: the present hexagram and approaching hexagram are read together, reconstituting the original moment of consultation with the wisdom of change. VINIT: the uncut bundle of 50 yarrow stalks. TANCH: the completed hexagram of 6 lines. AFWD: a yang line (solid). AREV: a yin line (broken). ENGAGR: the moment of change where a line is simultaneously old yang and young yin.

phase_0.domain_name: I Ching hexagram ob3ect: the 64 hexagrams of the Book of Changes, each composed of 6 lines that may be solid (yang) or broken (yin), with moving lines that transform. FSPLIT: yarrow stalk sorting splits the moment into moving lines (old yin, old yang) and static lines (young yin, young yang) — the moving lines generate a second hexagram from the first. FFUSE: the present hexagram and approaching hexagram are read together, reconstituting the original moment of consultation with the wisdom of change. VINIT: the uncut bundle of 50 yarrow stalks. TANCH: the completed hexagram of 6 lines. AFWD: a yang line (solid). AREV: a yin line (broken). ENGAGR: the moment of change where a line is simultaneously old yang and young yin.

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any consultation begins

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains the entire reading

AFWD.justification: forward morphism representing active, creative force

AREV.justification: reverse/descent morphism representing receptive, yielding force

CLINK.chosen_element: sequence of line transformations from first hexagram to second
CLINK.justification: composition chaining the morphisms of moving lines

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the hexagram itself as a self-referential symbol
IMSCRIB.justification: identity: the hexagram is what it is, recognized as itself

FSPLIT.justification: splits the moment into moving lines (old yin, old yang) and static lines (young yin, young yang)

FFUSE.chosen_element: reading the present hexagram and approaching hexagram together
FFUSE.justification: reconstitutes the original moment of consultation with the wisdom of change

ENGAGR.chosen_element: moment of change where a line is simultaneously old yang and young yin
ENGAGR.justification: paradox: both states simultaneously, the crux of transformation

IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible record of the consultation

phase_2.split_input: the moment of consultation (the question and the 50 stalks)
phase_2.fuse_element: reading the present hexagram and approaching hexagram together
phase_2.fuse_result: the original moment of consultation with the wisdom of change

phase_3.void_description: uncut bundle of 50 yarrow stalks before any sorting
phase_3.true_description: a hexagram with all yang lines (Heaven)
phase_3.false_description: a hexagram with all yin lines (Earth)
phase_3.both_description: a hexagram with moving lines that are both old yang and young yin

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the consultant recognizes the hexagram as a symbol of the current situation
Step 2 AREV: the consultant receives the yin lines as descent into receptivity
Step 3 FSPLIT: yarrow stalk sorting splits the moment into moving and static lines
Step 4 AFWD: the yang lines ascend as active forces
Step 5 FFUSE: the present and approaching hexagrams are fused to reconstitute the original moment
Step 6 CLINK: the sequence of line changes is composed into a narrative
Step 7 IFIX: the hexagram and its judgment are recorded permanently
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the hexagram is recognized again as the completed symbol of the consultation

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the I Ching text and its commentaries
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the interaction between the yarrow stalks and the consultant's intention
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the recorded hexagram and its judgment
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the ritual order of casting and reading
phase_5.alfs_store: the King Wen sequence of hexagrams

phase_6.cycle_cost: the yarrow stalks are consumed in the sorting process (one stalk set aside)
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: the uncut bundle of 50 stalks, the question in mind
phase_6.post_cycle_state: the recorded hexagram and its judgment, the stalks returned to bundle
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle returns to the bundle (minus one stalk) and the recorded wisdom, conserving the informational structure
